prose: Fukui reactivity indices computed for nitrobenzene (14 atoms: 2 O, 1 N, 6 C, 5 H) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Three separate RKS SCF calculations were run: neutral (charge 0, mult 1), cation/N−1 (charge +1, mult 2), and anion/N+1 (charge −1, mult 2). Fukui indices derived from Mulliken partial charges. Density fitting was OFF (exact four-centre integrals). Geometry used as-is from the user-supplied .xyz (no re-optimization at DFT level). All three SCFs converged; sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000. Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true. No warnings reported by the engine. Headline result: most electrophilic atom is C1 (ipso carbon, atom 4, 1-based), f+ = 0.49641781. Most nucleophilic atom is C2 (ortho carbon, atom 5), f− = 0.10729. The nitro group oxygens (atoms 1 & 2) also show high f+ (~0.473), and the para carbon (atom 9) shows elevated f+ (0.289), consistent with the electron-withdrawing, meta-directing nature of the nitro group. CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1. Output: nitrobenzene_fukui_dft.json. Bar chart: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260704-151209_nitrobenzene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
